sourceMovie.title: Forgotten
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Forgotten (2017) stuck because it commits to its drama premise without apologizing—viewers remember set pieces and momentum as much as plot. Lewis William Robinson's control of tone keeps the ride coherent. The film leans hard into its core genre, which is exactly what fans say they wanted more of from blockbusters in that era.

recommendations.title: Breathe
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Forgotten clicked for you, Breathe (2017) is a strong next stop: both trade in drama, with emotional whiplash that feels honest. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Forgotten?
faq.answer: Try Breathe, Time Is Up, Hands of Stone—each shares DNA with Forgotten while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Forgotten?
faq.answer: Forgotten (2017) is best described as Drama. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Forgotten worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 0.0/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this drama lane.
